Working with JavaScript Arrays

Arrays are a fundamental data structure in JavaScript. Here are some common patterns:

Mapping over arrays

const numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
const doubled = numbers.map(n => n * 2);
console.log(doubled); // [2, 4, 6, 8, 10]

Filtering arrays

const evens = numbers.filter(n => n % 2 === 0);
console.log(evens); // [2, 4]

Python example

def greet(name: str) -> str:
    return f"Hello, {name}!"

print(greet("world"))

These are the core functional programming patterns for working with arrays.
